spec_match.rationale: The page now uses the current SpeechRecognition.processLocally property plus SpeechRecognition.available() and install() option dictionaries instead of obsolete on-device helper names.

interactive.rationale: Users can probe one language, probe the full matrix, install a selected pack, compare local-only and remote-allowed statuses, and start a real recognition session when the API is present.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature card and page lede now frame v139 as a behind-a-flag developer trial with explicit language-pack availability checks and local-processing requests.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo handles missing SpeechRecognition, missing processLocally, missing available()/install(), permissions-policy state, offline state, unavailable/downloadable/downloading statuses, install errors, and language-not-supported recognition failures.

references.rationale: The concept cites the required chromestatus.com/feature/6090916291674112 link and the Web Speech API spec.

design_system.rationale: The page uses the shared CSS variables for colors, pairs every local button background with color, and avoids a static code-card-only layout.

summary: Rebuilt the availability matrix as a live lab for the current on-device Web Speech API: language and quality controls, local-vs-remote status comparison, install flow, microphone recognition path, fallback simulation, and updated v139 feature framing.
